screen.name: screen1
screen.instruct:
First, the blood must be diluted.
Use the pipette to fill the test tube with diluting solution.
screen.label: Diluting solution

screen.name: screen2
screen.instruct:
Good.
Now use the pipette to transfer a certain amount of the victim’s blood to the diluting solution in the test tube.

screen.name: screen3
screen.instruct: Use the special device, called a vortex mixer, to mix the sample.

screen.name: screen4
screen.instruct:
This is a so-called ELISA plate. Our plate contains 36 wells. Each drug is duplicated to increase the accuracy of the test.
Click on the well on the upper left to see an example of what’s inside.

In each of the 36 wells, specific antibodies are attached to the bottom.
The wells labeled “marijuana” contain antibodies against marijuana, the wells labeled “cocaine” contain antibodies against cocaine, and so on.
screen.caption: Well
Antibodies against marijuana

screen.name: screen5
screen.instruct:
In the wells labeled “positive control”, the different drugs have already been added to the wells.
Click on the well on the upper left again to see what happens.
When a sample containing the specific drug, in this case marijuana, is added to the well, the drug molecules bind to the antibodies.
screen.caption: Well
Antibodies against marijuana
Marijuana
Marijuana bound to antibodies

screen.name: screen6
screen.instruct: The wells labeled “negative control” contain just the antibodies. No drugs have been added to these wells.

screen.name: screen7
screen.instruct: Now you can use the pipette to add the diluted blood sample to the ELISA plate.

screen.name: screen8
screen.instruct:
The next step is to add a detection antibody to each well.
Use the pipette to add the detection antibody to the plate.
screen.label: Detection antibodies

screen.name: screen9
screen.instruct: Click on the upper left to see what happens with the detection antibody in the well.

The detection antibodies bind to the drugs that are bound to the antibodies at the bottom of the well.
If there are no drugs in the well, the detection antibodies will NOT bind.
screen.label: Detection antibodies
screen.caption: Well
Marijuana bound to antibodies
Detection antibodies
Drug-antibody complexes

screen.name: screen10
screen.instruct:
The last step is to add a reagent to each well.
Use the pipette to add the reagent to the plate.
screen.label: Reagent

screen.name: screen11
screen.instruct: Again, click on the well on the upper left to find out what the reagent does.

When the reagent binds to the drug-antibody complexes, it changes its color. In our case, it turns blue.
If there are no drugs in the sample, the reagent will NOT turn blue. It’s as simple as that.
screen.label: Reagent
screen.caption: Well
Drug-antibody complexes
Reagent
Drug-antibody complexes

screen.name: screen12
screen.instruct: Let’s see what the ELISA reveals.
screen.label: Reagent
